methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Hexium is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Charlie Jarrott and Jacob Peterson that has raised $10M+. The valuation landscape for nuclear energy includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using the $10M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $25M. This approach is grounded in observed step-ups within the early-stage venture funnel and holds particular relevance for a company at the threshold of Series A diligence. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while 8 nuclear energy companies populate the broader comp set, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier peers yielded insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3), and a sector-wide median would introduce material distortion given the wide dispersion in stage and maturity across nuclear ventures.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M (low) to $29M (high), with a median of $25M. This single-method triangulation reflects limited comps at Hexium's stage and sector specificity. Key uncertainties include technology readiness level relative to the broader nuclear cohort, capital intensity and runway implications of the current raise, and the timing and terms of Series A competitive dynamics within frontier nuclear.
